verdict.verdict_short_for_ken: GO — gemma4:26b is the operational default for OC2A local.
verdict.verdict_explanation: Across 25 test cases covering short/medium/long/complex/code prompts, explicit num_ctx 4096/8192/16384/32768, temperature 0.0 and 0.7, num_predict 20/80/200/500, and concurrent 2-request overlap, all 25 returned HTTP 200 with non-empty response and non-empty eval_count, including the previously broken num_predict=20 reasoning case that returned empty strings in TKT-1014. The think:false fix from TKT-1016 fully resolves the empty-response defect for gemma4:26b. Throughput is stable in the 47.9-88.6 t/s eval range (mean 58.6 t/s), cold load is 9.5s, warm load ~0.26s, and 16k-token context with 200-token generation still hits 47.9 t/s. The model is the clear default for OC2A local CREST Verify/governance workflows.

p4_num_predict_20_with_think_false.preview: The CAP theorem applies to distributed key-value stores like etcd or Consul because these systems operate over
The CAP theorem applies to distributed key-value stores like etcd or Consul because these systems operate over

regression_confirmation.fix_outcome: COMPLETE — think:false eliminates the empty-response bug for gemma4:26b at all num_predict values tested.

verdict.recommended_next_step: Promote gemma4:26b to the operational default for OC2A local CREST Verify / governance workflows. CHG-0959 (adopt gemma4:26b as local primary) is currently cancelled — re-open as CHG-0963 (or equivalent) with the post-fix verdict and route through Change Gate. All call sites that previously hit the empty-response bug must add top-level `"think": false` to /api/generate and /api/chat bodies. Keep OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL at default 1. The concurrent test confirms 2 overlapping requests work fine without raising it. gemma4:31b and gemma4:latest can stay pulled for ad-hoc higher-quality or fast/lightweight use; neither needs to be removed.
caveats: gemma4:26b is a 25.8B MoE (128 experts, 8 active) with Q4_K_M quantization; the 262144 default context is set but practical use is bounded by 48GB unified RAM. The harness used default ctx (262144) and explicit num_ctx up to 32768 — all passed without OOM.
Prompt-eval throughput is high (96-1040 t/s) on short prompts but compressed-context KV cache means very long prompts (16k) drop eval t/s to ~48.
The model emits </think>-style thinking tokens only when think=true; with think=false the response field contains clean output without preamble. Confirmed across all 25 tests.
Concurrent 2-request works without raising OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL (default 1) — requests serialize at the per-model queue but the second request still completes without OOM or error.

The think:false fix from TKT-1016 fully resolves the empty-response defect for gemma4:26b. Throughput is stable in the 47.9-88.6 t/s eval range (mean 58.6 t/s), cold load is 9.5s, warm load ~0.26s, and 16k-token context with 200-token generation still hits 47.9 t/s. The model is the clear default for OC2A local CREST Verify/governance workflows.
